---
title: About this site
---

The *Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society* is the longest-running
scientific journal in the world. Its first volume appeared in 1665, and its
back catalogue offers a remarkable window onto more than two centuries of
European natural philosophy and early science: experiments, astronomical
observations, letters from correspondents, and reports from the Society's
meetings. The articles indexed by this site span 1665–1886 — 8,545 items
across 25 broad topical labels.

As a part of the [_Secrets to Patent_](https://secretstopatents.org/) project,
we have crawled the publicly-available metadata for these articles from
royalsocietypublishing.org and assembled it into a single SQLite catalogue.
This site uses pre-computed aggregates of that catalogue to make topic
distributions explorable.

The current page set is a **prototype** focused on the *Experiment* topic
over time. Author productivity, page-length analysis, and a cross-filter
dashboard are planned next.

## What is and isn't published

This site publishes:

- Aggregate counts (articles per year, per topic, per topic × year).
- Per-article bibliographic metadata for items in the focus view: title,
  DOI, year, topic, and author names — together with a link to the original
  article on royalsocietypublishing.org.

This site does **not** publish:

- The raw SQLite metadata file.
- Any internal paths or URLs

## A note on author names

Author counts are based on the surface form of the name as stored in the
source metadata. No disambiguation is applied in this prototype, so the
same person under two spellings ("Newton, Isaac" vs. "Sir Isaac Newton")
appears as two distinct authors. Roughly 19 % of articles have no listed
author in the source.

## Tooling

The crawl, database build, derivative pipeline, and Quarto site were
assembled by Mathias Johansson using Python, SQLite, Quarto, Plotly,
GitHub Pages, and Claude.

## Citation

[![DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/DOI/10.5281/zenodo.20162091.svg)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20162091)

If you use this site or its derivative data in your work, please cite the
Zenodo deposit: [10.5281/zenodo.20162091](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20162091).

## License

[![CC BY-NC 4.0](https://licensebuttons.net/l/by-nc/4.0/88x31.png)](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc/4.0/)

This work is licensed under a
[Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International License](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc/4.0/)
(CC BY-NC 4.0).

## Contact

For questions or feedback, contact Mathias Johansson at
[MathiasJohansson@kultur.lu.se](mailto:MathiasJohansson@kultur.lu.se).
